title: salvadora persica var. indica
intro: Salvadora persica var. indica, commonly known as the Indian toothbrush tree, is a plant species native to the Indian subcontinent and parts of the Middle East. The plant is renowned for its medicinal properties, particularly the antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds found in its bark, which has been used for centuries to treat a range of health issues. The health benefits associated with Salvadora persica var. indica include its ability to reduce plaque and gum inflammation, prevent bad breath, and even alleviate symptoms of ulcers and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as toothpaste and mouthwashes, are often used to promote oral hygiene and overall health, while its bark can also be consumed as a powder or made into a decoction for internal use.
